$comment: Machine-readable surface for a future /apply-pattern installer. Human spec: patterns/fetch-on-webhook-reconcile/pattern.md. This pattern is expressed AS an invariant cluster (see docs/patterns-registry.md#patterns-as-clusters-of-invariants): the `invariants` array is the load-bearing field.
title: Fetch-on-Webhook Reconcile (non-monotonic external state)
applies_when: a signed webhook drives local state off an external system of record
the external state is NON-monotonic (can move backwards: active<->past_due, refunds, disputes)
stale / out-of-order / redelivered events must not mis-grant or mis-revoke
getting it wrong leaks revenue or corrupts entitlement (money path)
components: event-is-trigger-fetch-is-truth: decode payload only for ids, then fetch the live resource and project that; never read mutable state off the payload
retry-lease-dedupe: claim->processed lease on event id; release the claim on reconcile FAILURE so the provider redelivers; mark processed only on terminal success
single-writer-projection: exactly one path projects external truth onto the local field (check_single_writer.py enforceable)
unknown-id-fatal: unrecognized price/plan/sku errors + alerts + writes nothing; never a silent floor fallback
non-access-projects-to-floor: known-but-non-paying state grants the restrictive tier for enforcement (distinct from unknown-id error)
resource-mismatch-guard: bind fetched resource only if the record's cached id is empty or equal
terminal-deletion-latch: once the record is latched terminal, late events are ignored (not errored) so no state re-raises
rotation-list-signature: verify raw body against any currently-valid secret + bounded timestamp window
split-non-state-signals: refund/dispute-style events use the same lease but a no-projection audit-only effect

invariants.$comment: The cluster this pattern guarantees. Generic ids are the pattern's own; realized_as records the provenance app's app-specific id + code so /architect can pull the cluster with stable ids and the mining stays traceable to real code.

cluster.statement: Trigger-only: handler never reads mutable state from the payload; state comes from a live fetch.
Order-immune & idempotent; dedupe is a claim->processed lease that releases on failure for retry, not a silent drop.
Single-writer projection of the external-derived state.
Unknown external id is fatal: no write, no floor fallback, alert.
Known-but-non-access states project to the restrictive floor for enforcement.
Signature valid against a rotation list of secrets + bounded timestamp window.
Resource-mismatch guard: bind only if cached id empty or equal.
Terminal/deletion latch: late events ignored (not errored) once the record is latched terminal.

sibling_monotonic_branch.$comment: The FSM model for MONOTONIC state; captured so the selection criterion travels with the pattern.

cluster.statement: Terminal states never resurrect; advance-only FSM write, replay is a true no-op (target excluded from source set).

resource.description: External resource fetched for truth, e.g. subscription.

state_shape.description: Selects the FSM branch vs the fetch branch.

projected_field.description: Local field the reconcile is the single writer of.

unknown_id_policy.description: Present so a caller cannot silently weaken it.

signing_secrets.description: Rotation list the signature is checked against.

non_state_events.description: Events routed to the no-projection audit-only branch.

metrics.desc: wrong-state grant/revoke from out-of-order or stale events (target 0)
% of records whose local state equals a fresh live fetch

depends_on.provider-neutral-adapter: vendor + signed webhook sit behind the neutral seam

feeds.tiered-subscription: this IS its 'billing webhook is source of truth' component for the subscription resource
feeds.reconciliation-sweep: the periodic cousin; run both so the sweep catches events that never arrived
feeds.entitlement-overlay: reconcile recomputes max(payer, overlay) so it can never revert a comp; shared projected_field
